body:
Thank you for referring this lady who I reviewed in clinic today. She was referred with a six-week history of redness and irritation in the left eye.

On examination her visual acuity was 6/6 bilaterally. The findings were as follows:

- Right eye: quiet, no abnormality detected
- Left eye: mild diffuse conjunctival injection, 2+ anterior chamber cells, fine keratic precipitates inferiorly, no posterior synechiae
- Intraocular pressures: right 16mmHg, left 18mmHg
- Dilated fundoscopy: no vitritis, no retinal lesions bilaterally

The diagnosis is acute anterior uveitis of the left eye. I have commenced the following treatment:

- Dexamethasone 0.1% drops hourly to the left eye, tapering over six weeks
- Cyclopentolate 1% drops three times daily to the left eye for two weeks

I have requested the following investigations:

- HLA-B27
- ACE level
- Chest X-ray
- Syphilis serology

I will review Mrs Patel in two weeks. If her symptoms worsen before then, she should attend the eye casualty department.
